title: Referential Integrity Management for Sybase
text: Referential Integrity Management for Sybase By default, Uniface referential integrity is enabled. To use Sybase referential integrity you must set the dbms ri connector option in your assignment file. Note: Sybase does not allow the creation of DBMS referential integrity controls on views. These controls must be created on the base table or tables. Referential Integrity Controls Referential integrity can be maintained by the Sybase DBMS using a combination of primary key constraints, foreign key constraints, and triggers. Each individual relationship is controlled either by a foreign key constraint or by a group of triggers. Restrictive relationships are controlled by foreign keys and nonrestrictive ones are controlled by a group of triggers. An individual table may be in both restrictive and nonrestrictive relationships. In this situation, the table is controlled by both foreign key constraints and triggers, but each individual relation is controlled by either a foreign key constraint or by a trigger. Primary and Foreign Key Constraints The SYB connector creates a primary key constraint for all tables it creates so that the DBMS can understand the primary key definition. Sybase foreign key constraints can be used to maintain the referential integrity of restricted relationships. A foreign key constraint controls the following: Restrictive insert on the ‘many’ table. Restrictive update on both the ‘one’ and ‘many’ tables. Restrictive delete on the ‘one’ table. The foreign key constraint can be added to, or dropped from, the table using the Sybase SQL statement alter table . Relationships with cascading or nullify deletes cannot be controlled via a foreign key constraint. Therefore, Sybase insert, update, and delete triggers must be created for these types of relationships. Triggers When referential integrity is to be handled by Sybase, for nonrestrictive relationships, all inserts, updates, and deletes must be controlled via triggers. That is, for each relationship, triggers are used to control the entire relationship. Referential Integrity Violations When a restrictive relationship is violated, the transaction which caused the violation is rolled back, but the transaction state of the logon path is not modified. Therefore, users who want the transaction to be rolled back when referential integrity is violated must issue a rollback on the Uniface store trigger when the store fails. For example: trigger store store if ($status < 0) rollback endif end Copy When a trigger detects a violation of a restrictive referential integrity control, a Sybase rollback trigger command is issued. This command rolls back the work performed by the trigger and by the command which caused the Sybase trigger to fire. Data Conversion and Referential Integrity The procedure for data conversion into tables which participate in a relationship is different than data conversion into tables which are not in a relationship. To insert data into a table which participates in a relationship, either the data must be loaded in an order which preserves referential integrity at all times or the procedure described in the remainder of this section must be used.
